steps.action: Navigate to app at http://[::1]:3100 and select 'neurosynth' engine
steps.expected: Engine selected, predicate browser and Visual Query Builder visible with Undo/Redo buttons
steps.observed: Engine selected. Visual Query Builder appeared with Undo and Redo buttons in toolbar.

steps.expected: PeakReported added to query: 'Find v, v1, v2, v3 where PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3)'
steps.observed: PeakReported added. Query displays 'Find v, v1, v2, v3 where PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3)'. Datalog: 'ans(v, v1, v2, v3) :- PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3).'  'Remove PeakReported from query' button appeared.

steps.expected: PeakReported removed from query, query builder reverts to empty placeholder state
steps.observed: Query builder reverted to empty placeholder 'Click a predicate in the sidebar to start building your query...'. The 'Remove PeakReported from query' button disappeared from the interactive elements snapshot.

steps.action: After undoing PeakReported addition, click 'Redo last undone query step' button (@e49)
steps.expected: PeakReported restored to query: 'Find v, v1, v2, v3 where PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3)'
steps.observed: PeakReported restored. Query displays 'Find v, v1, v2, v3 where PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3)'. Datalog: 'ans(v, v1, v2, v3) :- PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3).' 'Remove PeakReported from query' button reappeared in interactive elements snapshot.

steps.action: Add PeakReported and Study predicates to query (no shared variables by default)
steps.expected: Both predicates visible in query builder with unique variable names
steps.observed: Query: 'Find v, v1, v2, v3, v4 where PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3) and Study(v4)'. All variables have unique names, no highlighting.

steps.action: Use QueryModel.connectVariable() via browser eval to make Study's param share variable 'v3' with PeakReported's last param. Trigger React refresh().
steps.expected: Variable 'v3' appears in both predicates and is highlighted with matching color
steps.observed: Query updated to 'Find v3 where PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3) and Study(v3)'. The variable 'v3' in all three positions (head, inside PeakReported, inside Study) received blue highlighting: color: rgb(37, 99, 235), border-color: rgb(37, 99, 235), background-color: rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.1). All three DOM elements had class 'vqb-var-token--shared'. The Datalog updated to 'ans(v3) :- PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3), Study(v3).' confirming the join.

evidence.domVerification: document.querySelectorAll('.vqb-var-token--shared').length === 3, all with style 'color: rgb(37, 99, 235); border-color: rgb(37, 99, 235); background-color: rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.1);'

frictions.description: The Vite dev server on port 3100 only listens on IPv6 (::1), not IPv4. agent-browser resolves 'localhost' to IPv4 first, causing 'Connection refused'. Had to use 'http://[::1]:3100' directly.
frictions.resolution: Used IPv6 URL http://[::1]:3100 directly instead of http://localhost:3100

frictions.description: Variable binding visualization (VAL-QBUILD-011) requires shared variable names across predicates. The default auto-generated variable names are always unique (v, v1, v2, ...) due to disambiguation logic, so no shared variable is created automatically. There is no UI mechanism exposed to rename or join variables between predicates.
frictions.resolution: Used browser eval to call QueryModel.connectVariable() directly to create a shared variable scenario, then triggered React refresh(). This verified the rendering logic works correctly. The implementation supports highlighting but lacks a user-facing variable-joining UI.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: all 3 passed. VAL-QBUILD-009: Undo button correctly removes the last added predicate and reverts to empty query state. VAL-QBUILD-010: Redo button correctly restores the previously undone predicate. VAL-QBUILD-011: Variable binding highlighting works correctly when shared variables exist — shared variable tokens receive the 'vqb-var-token--shared' CSS class and blue inline color/border styling. Note: the default variable name generation never produces shared variables automatically; a UI mechanism to join/rename variables is not yet implemented.
